channel.title: Work-Life Balance on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Work-Life Balance on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Staying Healthy While Developing Games
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about recent findings in fossilized endothermic theorapsids.

Now, these studies are really exciting because — oh, wait a minute, Nope, that’ll have to wait. I want to answer a question from a master member about staying healthy while developing games. This came from master member RoboCg, who literally asked this in my previous video about being happy, and he asked: “Do you have ideas about being healthy? From what I can see, a lot of my fellow game devs — mostly artists — are not having a healthy work life balance, which has caused some serious health rellated issues. Maybe you have some stories — Ergonomics might be a side theme for that. It feels like most people neglect it.

item.title: Being Happy
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about being happy.

Yeah, I just want to talk about being happy. It’s a fun Friday, It’s in the middle of June, It’s Pride month. Happy Pride everybody, and yes, I will mention being gay a little bit at the end of the video. Just to warn the Pearl Clutchers — especially the people who said I talk about being gay too much, which I checked — I’ve only had three Pride videos and very few videos where I talk about being gay out of over 500.. In fact, out of 565 videos, so I think you can handle it.

item.title: Avoiding Burnout
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about avoiding burnout and specifically want to talk about how I avoided burnout.

I’ve been asked this question a lot like multiple, times and for a long time — as long ago as eight months ago by Caleb zwit- 719 — and 5 days ago by Commander 67. The reason I haven’t posted anything about it is I wasn’t sure what my answer should be, because I have experienced down times — and I’ve talked about this — where I didn’t want to be in a lead role, I didn’t want to run a project, but I never, ever burned out in the sense that I get from other people about: you don’t want to do game development anymore, you want to leave the industry. I mean, I’m retiring and I’m still making my toys. So I thought about this for a long time and I I think I have an answer, but I don’t want to it to sound trit, so I’m going to tell you what my answer is and then I’m going to talk for a long time saying kind of what I mean by my answer. So I think I avoided burnout because I always had fun.

item.title: My Lost Decade
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my lost decade which is what I call 1993 to 2003, and I think

this is a pretty appropriate thing to talk about after I’ve talked about my whole work, life, balance, slash, passion, thoughts — and some people know this, some people don’t — but pretty much from the end of making Rags to Riches, which came out in 93, to almost the end of making tempo of Elemental Evil, which came out in 2003, I didn’t do anything except work on video games. Many of you may think, okay, he’s exaggerating. New. I pretty much worked every weekday, probably for about 10 hours until we, probably before the team started crunching, I always felt like I can’t ask people to Crunch, let me try to get some stuff done. So, starting about three to six months before I even think about asking anyone to put an extra time, I was working probably 10 to 12 hours a day and Saturday. So by the time crunch came around, I’d be working 12 to 14 hours a day every day, which meant I’ll talk about what that meant in terms of my life.

item.title: Work-Life Balance
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my thoughts on work-life balance in the game industry.

This is something that’s getting a lot of attention recently. A lot of people are talking about this, and I agree it’s a problem, so I’m just gonna — I’m gonna start and end this video saying this: work-life balance is important. I’ve noticed some people will watch my videos and walk away with very unusual takes on what I said, so I’m gonna make that clear. I started and stopped this video multiple times, and I’ve never done that. I usually sit down, think about what I want to say, do it, maybe bring up some notes to make sure I stay focused, but. This one had me starting and stopping and I finally realized what it was.
